# Phase 10.0.24 Repository Recovery Plan

Status: Planning-only. This plan does not create executable workflows, runtime authority, provider authority, infrastructure authority, deployment authority, app/runtime fixes, APIs, background jobs, automatic rollback systems, autonomous recovery systems, automatic branch repair, destructive repository operations, or execution authority.

## Objective

Define deterministic repository recovery, rollback legitimacy, reconstruction continuity, and source-control survivability after corruption, destructive merges, branch collapse, accidental mutation, or catastrophic repository failure.

## Repository Hygiene Posture

Current implementation posture: UNSTABLE.

Reason: unrelated runtime/application modifications, generated Prisma diffs, many untracked files, line-ending warnings, and branch ahead state exist, but no staged changes were detected during pre-build inspection.

## In-Scope Documents

- Constitutional repository-recovery doctrine.
- Deterministic rollback survivability philosophy.
- Repository reconstruction continuity framework.
- Branch-collapse recovery standards.
- Repository corruption containment doctrine.
- Constitutional rollback auditability architecture.
- Governance-aware repository restoration philosophy.
- Repository lineage reconstruction standards.
- Rollback integrity survivability doctrine.
- Phase 10.0.24 repository recovery summary.

## Validation Checklist

- `git status` inspected.
- `git diff` inspected.
- `git branch -vv` inspected.
- `git log --oneline --decorate -n 20` inspected.
- Staged changes inspected.
- Branch divergence inspected.
- Runtime/app drift reported.
- Generated artifacts reported.
- Untracked files reported.
- Repository state classified.
- Governance/runtime boundary integrity preserved.
- No app/runtime fixes performed.
- No cleanup, revert, reset, checkout, rebase, merge, branch repair, stage, or commit action performed.
- No runtime activation exists.
- No execution authorization exists.
- No deployment authorization exists.
- No provider onboarding exists.
- No orchestration activation exists.
- No infrastructure activation exists.

## Review Notes

- Repository recovery is evidence and governance only.
- Rollback legitimacy requires target, scope, evidence, user-work protection, boundary clarity, and reviewability.
- Reconstruction must label uncertainty rather than hide it.
- Do not include: runtime enablement, app/runtime fixes, providers, adapters, deployment paths, credentials, OAuth, APIs, fetch/axios, Prisma/Supabase changes, background jobs, rollback execution, automatic branch repair, autonomous recovery systems, destructive repository operations, or execution systems.
